arguments.prompt:
The user wants to see exactly what files would be generated if they create a Blazor Web App called "MyBlazorApp" in ./test-output with interactive server rendering. They do NOT want the project created yet — just a dry-run / preview of the file list.

Key details:
- Template: Blazor Web App (blazor)
- Project name: MyBlazorApp
- Output directory: ./test-output
- Interactive render mode: Server
- Do NOT actually create the project. Only show what files would be produced.

Please use `dotnet new blazor --dry-run` (or equivalent) to preview the output. Show the user the complete list of files that would be generated.

data.content:
Here's the complete dry-run preview. **No files were created** — this is purely what _would_ be generated.

---

### Command used
```
dotnet new blazor --name MyBlazorApp --output ./test-output --interactivity Server --dry-run
```

---

### Files that would be created (68 files)

#### Application Core (7 files)
| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `MyBlazorApp.csproj` | Project file |
| `Program.cs` | Application entry point |
| `appsettings.json` | Configuration |
| `appsettings.Development.json` | Dev-environment overrides |
| `Properties/launchSettings.json` | Launch/debug profiles |

#### Blazor Components (12 files)
| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `Components/App.razor` | Root application component |
| `Components/Routes.razor` | Router configuration |
| `Components/_Imports.razor` | Global `@using` directives |
| `Components/Layout/MainLayout.razor` | Main page layout |
| `Components/Layout/MainLayout.razor.css` | Layout styles (CSS isolation) |
| `Components/Layout/NavMenu.razor` | Side navigation menu |
| `Components/Layout/NavMenu.razor.css` | Nav menu styles |
| `Components/Layout/ReconnectModal.razor` | SignalR reconnection UI |
| `Components/Layout/ReconnectModal.razor.css` | Reconnect modal styles |
| `Components/Layout/ReconnectModal.razor.js` | Reconnect modal JS |
| `Components/Pages/Counter.razor` | Interactive counter demo page |
| `Components/Pages/Error.razor` | Error page |
| `Components/Pages/Home.razor` | Home/landing page |
| `Components/Pages/NotFound.razor` | 404 page |
| `Components/Pages/Weather.razor` | Weather data demo page |

#### Static Assets (2 files)
| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `wwwroot/app.css` | Application stylesheet |
| `wwwroot/favicon.png` | Favicon |

#### Bootstrap Library (47 files)
Full Bootstrap distribution under `wwwroot/lib/bootstrap/dist/` including:
- **CSS** (24 files) — `bootstrap.css`, grid, reboot, utilities — each with `.min`, `.rtl`, `.rtl.min` variants plus source maps
- **JS** (12 files) — `bootstrap.js`, `bootstrap.bundle.js`, `bootstrap.esm.js` — each with `.min` variants plus source maps

#### Post-creation action (automatic)
> `dotnet restore` would run automatically to restore NuGet packages.

---

### Key notes
- The **Server** interactivity mode means the `Counter.razor` page uses interactive server-side rendering via SignalR (Blazor Server), with the `ReconnectModal` component handling connection drops.
- When you're ready to actually create the project, just re-run the same command without `--dry-run`.

toolArgs.prompt:
The user wants to see exactly what files would be generated if they create a Blazor Web App called "MyBlazorApp" in ./test-output with interactive server rendering. They do NOT want the project created yet — just a dry-run / preview of the file list.

Key details:
- Template: Blazor Web App (blazor)
- Project name: MyBlazorApp
- Output directory: ./test-output
- Interactive render mode: Server
- Do NOT actually create the project. Only show what files would be produced.

Please use `dotnet new blazor --dry-run` (or equivalent) to preview the output. Show the user the complete list of files that would be generated.

data.content:
Here's what `dotnet new blazor --name MyBlazorApp --output ./test-output --interactivity Server --dry-run` would generate — **68 files total, nothing created yet**:

**App Core** — `MyBlazorApp.csproj`, `Program.cs`, `appsettings.json`, `appsettings.Development.json`, `Properties/launchSettings.json`

**Blazor Components** — `App.razor`, `Routes.razor`, `_Imports.razor`, layout files (`MainLayout`, `NavMenu`, `ReconnectModal` with `.css`/`.js`), and pages (`Home`, `Counter`, `Weather`, `Error`, `NotFound`)

**Static Assets** — `wwwroot/app.css`, `wwwroot/favicon.png`, plus **47 Bootstrap files** (CSS/JS with minified, RTL, and sourcemap variants)

**Post-action** — `dotnet restore` runs automatically.

The **Server** interactivity mode gives you SignalR-based interactive rendering with the `ReconnectModal` component for handling connection drops.

Say the word and I'll create it for real.
